_profile: Beginner SWING trader — Gold (XAUUSD). Not a day trader. Trades the 4H, judges trend on the Daily, holds positions for days. Lot size 0.01.
_strategy: Trend-pullback (the most common approach among experienced gold traders): use the EMA 200 to define the trend, then only enter in that direction when price pulls back to an EMA and RSI resets. Confirm, don't predict.

chart_indicators.EMA 200: Trend filter. Price ABOVE = uptrend (only look for buys). Price BELOW = downtrend (only look for sells).
chart_indicators.RSI (14): Momentum. Use GOLD zones 40/50/60 — NOT 30/70. In an uptrend RSI dips to 40-45 then turns up = buy trigger. In a downtrend RSI rallies to 55-60 then turns down = sell trigger.
chart_indicators.Volume: Confirmation. A signal candle on above-average volume is stronger.

bullish: Price is trading ABOVE the EMA 200 on the 4H (uptrend regime)
RSI(14) holds above 50; pullbacks bounce from the 40-45 zone and do not break below 40
Price structure shows higher highs and higher lows
Daily timeframe also shows price above its EMA 200 (trend agreement)
bearish: Price is trading BELOW the EMA 200 on the 4H (downtrend regime — this is the CURRENT state, price ~4,220 vs EMA200 ~4,483)
RSI(14) holds below 50; rallies stall in the 55-60 zone and do not break above 60
Price structure shows lower highs and lower lows
Daily timeframe also shows price below its EMA 200 (trend agreement)
neutral: Price is chopping right around the EMA 200 (flat / no clear trend)
RSI(14) is stuck between 45 and 55 with no momentum
No clean higher-high or lower-low structure — range-bound. Best action: WAIT, do not force a trade

entry_checklist: 1. TREND: Is price above (buy-only) or below (sell-only) the EMA 200? Trade only with it.
2. PULLBACK: Has price pulled back toward the EMA after moving away? Don't chase candles in the middle of a run.
3. RSI RESET: In an uptrend, has RSI dipped to ~40-45 and started turning up? (mirror for downtrends: ~55-60 turning down)
4. CONFIRMATION: Wait for a 4H candle to CLOSE in your direction before entering — no guessing mid-candle.
5. STOP & TARGET: Mark your stop (2x ATR or beyond the last swing) and target (1:2+ R:R) BEFORE you click.

position_sizing.what_it_means: 0.01 lot = 1 oz of gold. Every $1.00 move in price = $1.00 profit/loss. A 100-point ($100) move = $100.
position_sizing.stop_example: A 2x ATR stop on the 4H is currently ~$100 (ATR(14) ~$50). At 0.01 lot that risks ~$100 — only take it if that is <= 2% of your account balance. If not, your account is too small for a 0.01 swing stop; widen account or use a broker offering 0.001/nano lots.
position_sizing.rule: Keep size at 0.01 until you are profitable over at least 1 full month. Do not size up to recover a loss.

risk_rules: SWING ONLY: enter on the 4H, confirm trend on the Daily, hold for days. Do NOT scalp or watch 1m/5m charts.
Always set a Stop Loss BEFORE entering — every single trade, no exceptions.
Trade ONLY in the direction of the EMA 200. Above = buys on pullbacks. Below = sells on rallies.
Minimum 1:2 risk-to-reward. If you risk $100, the target must be $200 or more.
Risk at most 1-2% of account balance per trade.
Position size fixed at 0.01 lot until consistently profitable for 1+ month.
Max 2 open gold positions at once.
If you take 2 losing trades in a row, STOP for the day and review — no revenge trading.
Do NOT enter right before or during high-impact news (FOMC, US CPI, NFP) — gold whipsaws violently. Wait for it to settle.
When in doubt or the market is 'neutral', the correct move is to WAIT. No trade is a position.
current_market_read: As of this setup: 4H and Daily are BELOW the EMA 200 = downtrend regime. RSI(14) ~50 (neutral). Bias = look for SELLS on rallies into resistance (4,246 / 4,259), not buys. Earlier analysis flagged a sell zone 4,246-4,259 with stop ~4,280 toward 4,170/4,099. Do not buy against the trend unless price reclaims and holds above the EMA 200.
notes: Strategy researched from how experienced gold swing traders trade (trend-pullback with EMA 200 + RSI; sources: NordFX, LiteFinance, FXNX, ForexGDP, Mudrex). On-chart indicators are EMA 200 (trend) + RSI 14 (momentum, read with 40/60 gold zones). A richer custom indicator 'Gold Swing Pro — EMA 21/50/200 + Signals' (3-EMA ribbon + auto BUY/SELL pullback labels + ATR stops + trend tint) is SAVED in your TradingView account under Pine Editor > Recently used. To use it: open Pine Editor, load that script, click the play/Add-to-chart button, and remove one built-in indicator first (your Basic plan allows only 2 indicators per chart; upgrade to add more). Gold is highly volatile (4H ATR ~$50, Daily ~$115) — respect stops and keep size small while learning.
